sections.paragraph:
δυσμή, ῆς, ἡ the action of appearing to sink and so disappear from view, going down, setting (of the sun) (s. δύνω; Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, LXX), also as direction of the setting sun, west (Aeschyl. et al.) in our lit. (also LXX, En, TestSol 1:2 and 4 VW; TestJud 5:2; GrBar 8:1; Philo; Jos., e.g. Ant. 3, 199; 7, 16; SibOr, e.g. 4, 102; 5, 371 and 374; Just., D. 137, 4) exclusively pl., elsewh., nearly so (opp. ἀνατολαί) of east and west (BGU 1049, 8; Ps 106:3; TestJud 5:2) Mt 8:11; Lk 13:29; ἀπὸ δ. in the west Rv 21:13 (s. ἀπό 2a). ἐπὶ δυσμῶν in the west (cp. Num 22:1; 33:48; Dt 11:24, 30) Lk 12:54. Of lightning that flashes across the whole sky ἐξέρχεται ἀπὸ ἀνατολῶν καὶ φαίνεται ἕως δ. it comes fr. the east and shines to the west Mt 24:27 (cp. 1 Ch 12:16; Bar 4:37).—B. 871. DELG s.v. δύω. M-M.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. pl. = plural

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. fr. = from
  3. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 265
